message:
fix(guard): apply ignorecase to reservation pattern in path-conflict prefix checks

`check_path_conflicts` walks each active reservation and asks two
questions about the user-supplied `normalized` path:

  1. "is normalized a strict prefix of res.normalized_pattern?"
     (e.g. modifying `modules/submod` conflicts with reservation
     `modules/submod/**`)
  2. "is the literal base of a non-glob reservation a strict
     prefix of normalized?" (e.g. modifying `src/main.rs`
     conflicts with reservation `src/`)

Both branches were comparing the caller-side `normalized` string
(which the caller already lowercased when ignorecase was on)
against `res.normalized_pattern` *as stored* — i.e. with its
original case preserved. So a reservation registered with mixed
case would silently fail to match a lowercased path even when
ignorecase was supposed to be in effect.

Lower-case the pattern lazily inside the loop:
    let res_pattern = if ignorecase {
        res_pattern_lower = res.normalized_pattern.to_lowercase();
        &res_pattern_lower
    } else {
        &res.normalized_pattern
    };
and reuse `res_pattern` in both starts_with checks (replacing the
previous `&res.normalized_pattern` and the inner literal_base
re-borrow). The owned `res_pattern_lower` is declared outside the
`if` so the borrow it holds is valid for the rest of the loop
iteration. Pure case-insensitivity fix; no behavior change when
ignorecase is off.

.beads/issues.jsonl
  Routine sync. Adds bd-8qdh0 covering the known-regression
  archive batch-write performance gap (4x over budget), append-
  only — no rewrites of historical rows.

Untracked artifacts test_env_read and test_guard (4 MiB ELF
scratch binaries) intentionally not staged.
